Added a stats strip to every live and final game card:

  • Line score table — period-by-period scores (Q1–Q4 for NBA/WNBA/NFL, P1–P3 for NHL, innings 1–9 for MLB, 1H/2H for soccer; OT/SO appended as needed). MLB cards also get R/H/E columns.
  • Top performers — leading scorer per team (points/goals/passing yards depending on sport) with name and stat line.

Hidden for upcoming games. Applied to both desktop and mobile.

Claude
Editing ×2, Done, Fork verifier agent

Expanded leaders to a 3-column table per card:

  • NBA / WNBA: PTS · REB · AST
  • NFL: PASS · RUSH · REC
  • MLB: up to 4 categories (H · HR · RBI · AVG / pitcher stats as available)
  • NHL: P · G · A (or saves for goalies)
  • Soccer (EPL/NWSL): G · A · SV (or rating)

Each row shows the stat label and both teams' top performer side-by-side with their name + value. Falls back gracefully when ESPN provides fewer categories.

Bundled to a single offline file. Same caveat as before: live scores, news, and draft data come from the ESPN API at runtime, so those panels need an internet connection to populate — UI, fonts, and structure are fully self-contained.
